markdown:
#  Create or Link Coordination Issues on a Drawing 
When you create a coordination issue on a drawing in Procore, the system automatically adds a snapshot and a link to that spot. This link gives your project team the context they need to learn about and fix the issue.
##  Things to Consider 
  * [Required User Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/permissions-matrix/project-level-coordination-issues-permissions#:~:text=Create%20or%20Link%20Coordination%20Issues,permissions%20on%20the%20Drawings%20tool.)
  * **Additional Information:**
    * Creating a new issue or linking an existing one automatically adds the markup to the drawing for anyone with access to the Drawings tool.
    * When you create a coordination issue, it automatically includes a snapshot and a link to the original drawing. This helps users easily reference the drawing from the Coordination Issues tool.
    * When using the Pin method, the color of the pin indicates the status of the linked issue:
      * **Blue:** Open or Released
      * **Yellow:** Ready for Review
      * **Red:** Elevated to an RFI
      * **Gray:** Closed


##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to the project's **Drawings** tool.
  2. Find the drawing to markup. If your team is using Drawing Areas, you may need to open the area first. 
  3. Click the **Drawing No**. link to open it. 
  4. In the top navigation, click the **Markup** icon. This opens the markup toolbar. 
  5. Continue as follows:


#### To pin coordination issues to a drawing:
  1. Click **Pins** ![Drawings Toolbar Pin](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/b85e3462c6b070d43f981e592f7dd3ea/561f081bd6aac4bebef6684780b608f0/drawings-toolbar-pin.png?fm=webp&h=20) in the markup toolbar.   

##### Tip
**Don't see the Pins icon?** If **Pins** isn't visible, another pin type (like Photos or Punch) might be active. Click that icon to expand the menu and then click **Issues**. See [Mark Up a Drawing](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/tutorials/mark-up-a-drawing).
  2. Click the area on the drawing where you want the pin to appear. A shortcut menu appears.
     * **To create a pin and link it to a new issue** :
       1. Choose **Create New Coordination Issue** to open the side panel.
       2. Complete the issue's required fields. Learn about each field in [Create Coordination Issues on a Drawing](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/coordination-issues-project/tutorials/create-coodination-issues-on-a-drawing).
       3. Click **Create**.  
Procore pins the issue to the drawing and adds it to the Coordination Issues tool. Click **Publish** to share the pin with other users. Once published, the system automatically creates a snapshot.
     * **To link a pin to an existing issue** :
       1. Choose **Link to Existing Coordination Issue** to open the side panel.
       2. Search for and select the issue you want in the selection box. You can double-click this box to reveal a full list of your project's coordination issues. 
       3. (Optional) To share the pin with others, click **Publish.**


#### To add or link a coordination issue to another markup type:
  1. Click an icon on the markup toolbar (such as **Cloud** , **Arrow** , or **Text**). 
  2. Click the area on the drawing where you want the markup to appear. 
  3. Click the **Paperclip** ![Icon Link Item](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/b232902e86447bbdd16b569625f86143/06d7a025357ad7eef896718f46ed98ab/icon-link-item.png?fm=webp&h=16) icon and choose **Coordination Issue** from the shortcut menu.  
![Link Coordination Issue](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/219c808e9dd743a5d3eb1cc426758135/523df612aaf0b07b612fe49a08245c5a/link-coordination-issue.png?fm=webp)
     * **To create a new coordination issue for the selected markup type** :
       1. Choose **Create New Coordination Issue** to open the side panel.
       2. Complete the issue's required fields. Learn about each field in [Create Coordination Issues on a Drawing](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/coordination-issues-project/tutorials/create-coodination-issues-on-a-drawing).
       3. Click **Create**.  
Procore pins the issue to the drawing and adds it to the Coordination Issues tool. Click **Publish** to share the pin with other users. Once published, the system automatically creates a snapshot.
     * **To link an existing coordination issue to the selected markup type** :
       1. Choose **Link to Existing Coordination Issue** to open the side panel.
       2. Search for and select the issue you want in the selection box. You can double-click this box to reveal a full list of your project's coordination issues. 
       3. Click the coordination issue to link it to the drawing markup.
       4. (Optional) To share the pin with others, click **Publish.**


## See Also
  * [Mark Up a Drawing](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/tutorials/mark-up-a-drawing)
  * [What are custom fields and which Procore tools support them?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-are-custom-fields-and-which-procore-tools-support-them)
  * [What are configurable fieldsets and which Procore tools support them?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-are-configurable-fieldsets-and-which-procore-tools-support-them)
title: Create or Link Coordination Issues on a Drawing
description: How to link or create coordination issues on a drawing.
